items.headline: City approves Seeds of Peace Monument artwork at Guadalupe River Park
items.summary: The City Council approved an agreement with the Diane Brandenburg Survivor's Trust to install a Seeds of Peace Monument, an artwork by artist Mario Chiodo, at Guadalupe River Park Arena Green West. The work memorializes a local survivor and adds a new public art installation to the riverfront park.

items.headline: Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District sets FY 2026-27 budget
items.summary: The City Council took action on the Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District's 2026-2027 budget and annual assessment. The district, which covers a commercial area on the city's east side, formally set its spending plan for the upcoming fiscal year.

items.headline: Cupertino updates Health and Safety element of General Plan
items.summary: The City Council reviewed updates to the Health and Safety Element of the Cupertino General Plan (2015–2040 Community Vision). This element addresses how the city plans to protect residents and manage public health and safety concerns over the next 15 years.

items.headline: City Council appoints new permanent City Manager
items.summary: The City Council adopted a resolution appointing a permanent City Manager. This fills the top administrative position in city government, responsible for day-to-day operations and implementing council policies.

items.headline: New duplex permitted on Old Piedmont Road in San José
items.summary: A permit has been issued for a new two-story, two-unit duplex at 2174 Old Piedmont Road, valued at over $1 million. This represents one of the larger residential projects in this batch and adds to the city's housing stock beyond single-family homes.

items.headline: Sunnyvale updates water shortage plans amid drought concerns
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission reviewed the Draft 2025 Urban Water Master Plan and Water Shortage Contingency Plan on May 18. The contingency plan outlines how the city will manage water supplies if shortages occur.

items.headline: City awards 2026 community grants in spring cycle
items.summary: The Council considered applications for the 2026 Community Events and Neighborhood Grant Program on May 18, distributing funds to support local community events and neighborhood improvements.

items.headline: City to develop principles for residential electrification programs
items.summary: Palo Alto is developing proposed principles to guide residential electrification programs aimed at helping the city comply with regional air quality regulations. The council will hear a status update and staff presentation on May 15.
